name: IEC 62443-3-3
fullName: Industrial Communication Networks - System Security Requirements
description: Defines security capabilities for industrial automation and control systems (IACS) including zone and conduit protection.

questions.name: IEC 62443-3-3 - Unique Human User Identification
questions.text: Are all human users uniquely identified and authenticated before accessing IEC 62443-3-3 systems (utilizing secure Jump Hosts, MFA validation nodes, active directory GPOs, and hardware tokens)?
questions.description:
Verify unique operator ID validation gates across all SCADA interfaces, HMIs, and Level 2/3 engineering workstations.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.
4. Document the Security Level achieved (SL-A) vs target requirements (SL-T) for each zone conduit.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the identification & authentication configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: Evaluation evidence must include: Zone and Conduit design architecture diagram, Security Level Target (SL-T) vs Security Level Achieved (SL-A) matrix, and OEM device integration manual validation registers.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Unauthenticated or unmonitored IT-OT bridge endpoints can expose critical IEC 62443 3 3 systems to lateral network pivoting. An administrative compromise in the enterprise domain (such as phishing or AD account compromise) can lead directly to unauthorized SCADA control commands.

questions.name: IEC 62443-3-3 - Unique Software Process Vetting
questions.text: Are all software processes uniquely identified and authenticated on IACS controllers (utilizing secure Jump Hosts, MFA validation nodes, active directory GPOs, and hardware tokens)?
questions.description:
Ensure process signatures, execution controls, and whitelist guards restrict unauthorized code execution on PLCs.

SOP:
1. Establish physical locking covers and secure enclosures around critical field device interfaces.
2. Configure hardware configuration locks and disable local diagnostic ports (USB, RS-232) to block local unauthorized adjustments.
3. Validate that device configuration changes require double-signature supervisor tokens before logical modifications are written to memory.
4. Document the Security Level achieved (SL-A) vs target requirements (SL-T) for each zone conduit.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the identification & authentication configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: Evaluation evidence must include: Zone and Conduit design architecture diagram, Security Level Target (SL-T) vs Security Level Achieved (SL-A) matrix, and OEM device integration manual validation registers.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Unauthenticated or unmonitored IT-OT bridge endpoints can expose critical IEC 62443 3 3 systems to lateral network pivoting. An administrative compromise in the enterprise domain (such as phishing or AD account compromise) can lead directly to unauthorized SCADA control commands.

questions.name: IEC 62443-3-3 - Multi-Factor Authentication
questions.text: Is multi-factor authentication enforced for remote connections to IEC 62443-3-3 components (utilizing secure Jump Hosts, MFA validation nodes, active directory GPOs, and hardware tokens)?
questions.description:
Check MFA configurations utilizing hardware tokens for external remote support tunnels and Jump Host mediation.

SOP:
1. Enforce strict role-based access controls (RBAC) separating administrative tasks from standard operator routines.
2. Route all incoming remote connections through isolated administrative Jump Hosts with visual session logging active.
3. Conduct quarterly access audits to identify and completely disable dormant or inactive accounts.
4. Document the Security Level achieved (SL-A) vs target requirements (SL-T) for each zone conduit.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the identification & authentication configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: Evaluation evidence must include: Zone and Conduit design architecture diagram, Security Level Target (SL-T) vs Security Level Achieved (SL-A) matrix, and OEM device integration manual validation registers.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Unauthenticated or unmonitored IT-OT bridge endpoints can expose critical IEC 62443 3 3 systems to lateral network pivoting. An administrative compromise in the enterprise domain (such as phishing or AD account compromise) can lead directly to unauthorized SCADA control commands.

questions.name: IEC 62443-3-3 - Authorization Enforcement
questions.text: Is role-based authorization configured to restrict IACS setpoint controls?
questions.description:
Audit RBAC policies limiting execution of sensitive commands, firmware flashing, and logic adjustments to certified operator profiles.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.
4. Document the Security Level achieved (SL-A) vs target requirements (SL-T) for each zone conduit.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the use control configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: Evaluation evidence must include: Zone and Conduit design architecture diagram, Security Level Target (SL-T) vs Security Level Achieved (SL-A) matrix, and OEM device integration manual validation registers.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
General IT-OT convergence increases the threat landscape by bridging air-gapped industrial facilities with internet-facing corporate systems. Failing to enforce strict regulatory controls risks introducing severe operational vulnerabilities.

questions.name: IEC 62443-3-3 - Message Integrity
questions.text: Is communication integrity protected using cryptographic signatures on IACS buses (covering Siemens S7-1500 PLCs, Allen-Bradley ControlLogix, SEL RTUs, and digital relay modules)?
questions.description:
Verify integrity checks and signatures on Modbus/TCP or DNP3 protocol packets traversed over the production bus.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.
4. Document the Security Level achieved (SL-A) vs target requirements (SL-T) for each zone conduit.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the system integrity configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: Evaluation evidence must include: Zone and Conduit design architecture diagram, Security Level Target (SL-T) vs Security Level Achieved (SL-A) matrix, and OEM device integration manual validation registers.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Using unhardened or unpatched field controllers opens critical hardware interfaces to remote execution exploits. Attackers can leverage known vulnerabilities to flash unauthorized firmware or change safety threshold parameters on active PLCs.

questions.name: IEC 62443-3-3 - Cryptographic Encryption
questions.text: Is cryptographic encryption enforced for all IACS data transit (utilizing VPN tunnels, encrypted Modbus/DNP3 secure protocols, and HSM keys)?
questions.description:
Audit SSL/TLS configurations and VPN encryption algorithms traversing physical security zones between Level 2 and Level 3.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.
4. Document the Security Level achieved (SL-A) vs target requirements (SL-T) for each zone conduit.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the data confidentiality configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: Evaluation evidence must include: Zone and Conduit design architecture diagram, Security Level Target (SL-T) vs Security Level Achieved (SL-A) matrix, and OEM device integration manual validation registers.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Traversing industrial telemetry in cleartext across converged networks invites eavesdropping and packet injection. Malicious actors can execute Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) attacks, spoofing HMI screens while sending dangerous control commands.
